commit.message:
Crawl action items in cmd_crawl with 60-day lookback

Previously cmd_crawl only extracted birthdays. Action items were only
crawled during the daily digest with a 3-day window, so the bot only
ever had this week's items. Now:
- cmd_crawl does both birthdays (2yr) and action items (60 days)
- Daily digest crawl widened from 3 to 14 days for ongoing coverage
- Blocked sender filtering applied to both pipelines

commit.message:
Bisection drawdown : capital épuisé exactement à ageFin

Le drawdown était calculé par formule d'annuité avec un taux blend fixe,
mais la simulation applique des rendements distincts par enveloppe avec
des drags fiscaux différents. Résultat : dérive cumulative (ex: 426k€
de résidu à 80 ans au lieu de 0).

Fix : simulateDrawdownFinal() réplique la logique capital des phases 2-3
(croissance par enveloppe, retrait pro-rata, IS drag, PER débloqué à 64).
Bisection (50 itérations max, convergence à 1€) sur cette simulation
pour trouver le drawdown exact. La formule d'annuité reste comme borne
haute initiale.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): fix auth redirect loop after login

Two issues fixed:
1. Renamed proxy.ts → lib/auth-middleware-draft.ts to prevent it from
   being compiled as Next.js middleware. It was causing 307 redirects
   from /dashboard to /auth?redirect= because the cookie check always
   fails (cookies are on the API domain, not the web domain).

2. Dashboard layout had a zustand persist hydration race condition:
   isAuthenticated defaults to false before localStorage rehydration,
   causing immediate redirect to /auth. Added hydration gate to wait
   for persist rehydration before checking auth state.

commit.message:
Fix base SCPI (réinvestissements) + prévoyance phase 2 + documenter limitations

1. Base comptable SCPI : les distributions réinvesties (après IS et frais
   de souscription) augmentent maintenant la base. Évite la double taxation
   partielle à la cession (~3-5% d'erreur corrigée sur la fiscalité SCPI).

2. Prévoyance ajoutée aux frais phase 2 (levée de pied) : la SASU maintenue
   avec missions ponctuelles garde la prévoyance (~3k€/an).

3. Documenter les limitations assumées :
   - Forfait TME déduit du contrat capi (pas de la tréso SASU)
   - IR foyer résiduel non modélisé en phases 2-3

fix: resume-from-checkpoint + persist 429 state across restarts

Problem: container restart always re-fetches same accounts (insertion order),
@sama first → immediate 429 → 200+ accounts never reached.

Fix 1 (db.py): ORDER BY last_fetched_at ASC NULLS FIRST
- NEVER-fetched accounts always go first
- After restart, accounts already done this cycle sort to back naturally

Fix 2 (db.py + scheduler.py): persist rate_limit_reset_at in scraper_state
- On 429: write reset timestamp to DB, clear after normal sleep
- On startup: if reset window still active, sleep remaining time first
- Log: [CYCLE] Rate limit carry-over from previous run, sleeping Xs

commit.message:
Fix core icon_url not appearing: add missing field to toCore mapper

The toCore() function explicitly maps all fields but was missing icon_url,
so the DB value was silently dropped despite SELECT * fetching it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ingest): reduce CDN fetch timeout and remove redundant second call

CDN fetch was 180s × 3 retries = 9 min, called twice = 18 min of
blocking. Signal Desktop became unresponsive processing SSL errors on
hundreds of attachments, causing _get_desktop_messages to time out and
the entire job to fail despite successful sync.

Reduced to 30s × 1 attempt. Removed redundant second CDN fetch call.
Increased message fetch timeout to 180s for large groups.

commit.message:
fix(smith): Copilot CLI does not support stdin prompt via -p -

Copilot CLI's -p flag only accepts literal text, not stdin. The smith
was passing -p - which copilot interpreted as the prompt being a dash
character. Now copilot prompts are passed directly as -p <text> via
BuildArgsWithPrompt, while claude/gemini continue using stdin.

commit.message:
test(e2e): add SAB grab-flow e2e via Prowlarr fixture (Task 7)

Prowlarr fixture server now implements:
- GET /<indexerId>/download: records grab (mode=redirect param simulates
  redirect-only grab for false-positive path), returns 200
- GET /api/v1/history: returns stateful releaseGrabbed record with
  downloadClientName (direct) or without (redirect), enabling
  triggerProwlarrGrab verification loop to resolve deterministically

Fixture releases now carry valid fixture-origin downloadUrls
(http://127.0.0.1:<port>/1/download?guid=...) so isSafeProwlarrDownloadUrl
passes. One usenet release added for send-to-sab protocol coverage.

New scout.spec.cjs tests:
- send-to-sab succeeds for usenet fixture release -> queued:true
- send-to-sab returns prowlarr_grab_unsubmitted on redirect-only grab -> 502

Suite: 49/49 pass (~10s).

commit.message:
feat(ui): add Linux native GTK4 backend with reactive flow fixes and ui.html widget

Introduce GTK4-based native UI rendering alongside the existing terminal and
browser backends. The reactive flow engine now caps update cycles at 255 to
prevent infinite loops. The RSS reader example is refactored to use individual
single-output event handlers instead of a multi-output Update func, fixing
state corruption from multi-emit returns. New ui.html() widget renders HTML
content via Pango markup in GTK and passes through raw HTML in the browser.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: content search on Desktop misrouted to filename search

Add grep example for Desktop to anchor "has the word X in it" queries
to grep -ril instead of find -iname. The LLM had two Desktop examples
both using find -iname, and the only grep example said "in documents"
not "on desktop" — so it always pattern-matched Desktop → find.

Also: yes, 17 files on your Desktop contain the word "language"
(wp_fb.html, bookmarks_10_04_2021.html, a.html, and others).

commit.message:
fix(deploy): restore standalone, override start with next start

Reverting standalone removal (broke RAILPACK build). Instead, keep
standalone for RAILPACK auto-detection but override startCommand in
railway.toml to use `npx next start` which correctly serves static
files. Removed builder/buildCommand since RAILPACK handles builds.
Fixed CWD assumption (removed `cd apps/web` since Root Directory
is already apps/web).

commit.message:
Day 20: Closed-world reasoning, copilot tools, visibility scoping

- Rewrite reasoning system prompt for closed-world model: evidence-only
  reasoning, governance policies as hard blockers, evidence-based output
  schema (evidenceSummary, evidenceFor/evidenceAgainst)
- Add post-reasoning policy verification in reasoning engine to catch
  LLM proposing blocked/unpermitted actions, override to null
- Query Operator record for company name in reasoning prompt
- Add get_operational_briefing copilot tool: department-grouped situation
  summary with period filter (today/week/month)
- Add search_department_knowledge copilot tool: RAG search across visible
  department documents with graceful fallback
- Rewrite get_department_context tool: fix digital entity count via
  department-member relationships, show active situations
- Thread visibleDepts through all copilot tool executions; scope
  get_org_structure, list_departments, create_situation_type
- Update situations page: evidence summary display, backward-compatible
  rendering for old pros/cons and new evidenceFor/evidenceAgainst formats

commit.message:
fix(idea-machina): fix broken JSX nesting in CoreSummaryCard icon layout

The icon image and flex wrapper divs were improperly nested, causing the
entire card to render incorrectly. Fixed div hierarchy so icon displays
alongside core name/description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix video/file-type search and ls sort header bug

- Add sys1 examples for finding videos/images across device using find -ls
  with proper extension grouping and sort -k7 -rn (macOS-compatible)
- Add reformatOutput handler for find -ls output: parses inode/size/path
  columns and converts byte counts to human-readable (K/M/G), handles
  filenames with spaces via f[10...].joined()
- Fix -S flag detection in ls reformatOutput: cmd.contains("-S") was false
  for combined flags like -lhS (no literal "-S" substring). Now extracts
  the flags token and checks for uppercase S within it
- Fix Swift escape: \( in triple-quoted string was parsed as interpolation;
  changed to \\( / \\) to produce literal shell \( \) in the prompt
- Fix sort flag: sort -rh is GNU-only; use sort -rn for macOS compatibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Jenkins validation pipeline support (v0.15.0)

New variables: validation_jenkins_job, backend_repo_url, backend_image_name,
backend_jenkins_job, frontend_jenkins_job, validation_image_name,
validation_credential_id. When validation_jenkins_job is set:
- Jenkinsfile builds without :latest, archives metadata, triggers validation
- Jenkinsfile.validation runs tests in K8s Job, promotes images on success
- Optional credential injection for test secrets (S3, OIDC, etc.)
- Dockerfile.validation and validation-entrypoint.sh for test container

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(channel): send ECLAW_READY init push to bot after full bind

On successful channel bind, immediately fire-and-forget an ECLAW_READY
system push containing deviceId/entityId/botSecret credentials and
pre-built curl templates for Mission Dashboard tools, so the bot has
full context without waiting for a user message.
